title: Protocol
description: A defined set of rules for exchanging data across a boundary — the contract a Touchpoint speaks. Well-known protocols (https, grpc, mcp, ...) are referenced by name; when a boundary has no existing protocol, a Protocol is DEFINED here: its message format, version, and a link to its spec/contract. This is where "if no protocol exists, we define one" becomes a concrete, reusable asset.

version.description: Protocol/contract version, e.g. HTTP/2, gRPC v1, MCP 2025-06.

format.description: Wire format: json | jsonld | protobuf | avro | csv | form | binary.

spec.description: Link to the defining spec/contract (OpenAPI, .proto, JSON Schema, RFC).

status.description: standard | custom | draft — 'custom' marks a protocol newly defined for an uncovered boundary.
